name: Electronic Excited States (TD-DFT)
description: Excited states are computed from the ground state with time-dependent DFT: solving the Casida equations yields excitation energies and oscillator strengths, selection rules classify transitions as bright or dark, and Franck–Condon factors shape the simulated UV-Vis spectrum.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Ground-state wavefunction"]
    N2["Apply TD-DFT response"]
    N3["Solve Casida equations"]
    N4["Excitation energies + f"]
    N5{"Symmetry-allowed?"}
    N6["Forbidden: dark state"]
    N7["Allowed: bright state"]
    N8["Franck–Condon structure"]
    N9["Simulate UV-Vis spectrum"]
    N10["Assigned transitions"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|no| N6
    N5 -->|yes| N7
    N6 --> N9
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff

sources.title: Density-Functional Theory for Time-Dependent Systems
sources.authors: Runge, E.; Gross, E. K. U.

sources.title: Time-dependent density functional response theory of molecular systems (Casida)
sources.journal: Recent Advances in Density Functional Methods

sources.title: Molecular Quantum Mechanics, 5th ed. — Molecular Spectroscopy
sources.authors: Atkins, P. W.; Friedman, R. S.
